name: Office of Co-operative Societies of Papua New Guinea (OCS PNG)
description: The OCS PNG was established by the Government of Papua New Guinea in 2000 to revitalise and facilitate the development of co-operative societies in the country. They are therefore the lead agency providing funds to co-operatives, coordinating trainings, opening up market access, administering the Co-operative Societies Act and registering co-operatives. OCS PNG is headed by the Registrar of Co-operative Societies who reports to the Minister for Trade, Commerce & Industry. They are supported by this Minister for administrative matters.
